specialty: General Medicine/ Pediatric medical management/ General surgery
min_doctor_qualification: The patient should be periodically seen by a nephrologist to decide the dialysis prescription and line of treatment
clinical_indications: Diagnosis of Acute Kidney Injury or Chronic Kidney Disease backed by clinical signs, symptoms and investigations
Acute Kidney Injury with clinical features such as reduced urine output, dark concentrated urine, swelling of face and limbs, breathlessness
Acute Kidney Injury with investigations showing elevated serum creatinine, electrolyte/acid-base abnormalities, supportive imaging and serological findings
Chronic Kidney Disease with clinical features such as Edema, Hematuria, Proteinuria, renal stone, family history of kidney diseases, unexplained hypertension, Anemia
Chronic Kidney Disease with investigations showing elevated serum creatinine, supportive imaging findings
Need to create space for fluids or blood products via dialysis

preauth.label: Pathological Examination (Complete Blood count, Blood urea, Serum Creatinine, GFR, serum electrolytes; monthly for chronic dialysis, quarterly for Serum Iron, ferritin, TIBC, TSAT, SGOT, SGPT, viral markers, calcium, phosphate)

claim.label: Detail dialysis chart (only dialysis chart in chronic dialysis pts)

ppd_preauth.q: Was the patient a confirmed case of ARF?
Did Renal Function Test show Raised Sr. creatinine?
Are detailed vital parameters chart available including Input/output chart?
Initial diagnosis has been confirmed as chronic kidney disease and end-stage kidney failure?
Maximum 3 dialysis sessions being done per week in a single pre-authorisation?

cpd_claim.q: Clinical Notes with all the details of the dialysis done?

common_queries: Clinical note does not mention the need for dialysis
Diagnosis of Acute Kidney Injury or Chronic Kidney disease/End-stage kidney disease not mentioned
Pathological examination reports (CBC, Blood urea, Serum Creatinine, GFR, serum electrolytes) missing or not repeated monthly/quarterly as required for chronic cases
Dialysis chart (for chronic dialysis patients) missing or incomplete
More than 3 dialysis sessions per week requested in a single pre-authorization without separate pre-auth
eGFR threshold not met or not documented for CKD dialysis

additional_information.special_empanelment: Dialysis facilities available, Dialysis machines – for general unit, water treatment plant, separate isolation area for dialyzing patients with hepatitis B, dialysis reprocessing area, Working hours of unit per day
additional_information.min_doctor_qualification_desirable: MD/ DNB (Medicine); DM/ DNB (Nephrology) or equivalent
clinical_key_pointers: Proceed with Haemodialysis only if diagnosis made is backed by clinical signs, symptoms and investigations.
A patient with pre-existing CKD may develop AKI
referral_criteria: Initial evaluation of all newly diagnosed cases (CKD)
contraindications: Look for contraindications to HD or PD
